methods.description: This method involves analyzing pollen type occurrences across different climatic zones using various statistical techniques to understand the distribution and characteristics of pollen types.

steps.description: The number of pollen type occurrences per climatic zone was computed and displayed as a heatmap with two-way clustering analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: compute pollen type occurrences and create heatmap with clustering

steps.description: Based on a contingency table summarizing the number of pollen types present at each climatic zone, a correspondence analysis (CA) was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform correspondence analysis on pollen contingency table

steps.description: The variation in occurrence odds of each pollen grain shape among the bioclimatic zones was tested using a generalized linear model (GLM) with a binomial distribution error and logit link.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GLM to test pollen grain shape occurrence odds

steps.description: The effects of latitude, longitude, and altitude on the occurrence of each pollen grain shape were tested using a generalized linear mixed-effect model (GLMM).
steps.searchable_summary: use GLMM to test effects of geo-coordinates on pollen occurrence

steps.description: Similarity of pollen morphotypes between climatic zones was analyzed qualitatively and quantitatively using the Jaccard coefficient and Bray-Curtis distance.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen morphotype similarity using Jaccard and Bray-Curtis

steps.description: Shared and zone-specific pollen shapes among the climatic zones were displayed using a seven-set Venn diagram.
steps.searchable_summary: create Venn diagram for shared and specific pollen shapes

steps.description: Statistical analyses were carried out using the R packages d3heatmap, nlme, and venn.
steps.searchable_summary: use R packages for statistical analysis
